About: It is about the Battle of Antietam during the American Civil War.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Blue & Gray: Four American Civil War Battles.
It is associated with the Blue & Gray Folio Series.
ConsistsOf: It consists of a small map and only 100 counters.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by SPI's 1972 game Napoleon at War.
KnownFor: It is known for being one of SPI's most popular games in the year following its publication.
It is known for its simple rules.
It is known for a special rule that limited the number of Union units that could move.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the American Civil War.
PartOf: It is part of the four-game collection Blue & Gray: Four American Civil War Battles.
PublishedAs: It was published as a stand-alone "folio" game.
It was published as an individual game packaged in a double LP-sized cardstock folio.
PublishedBy: It was published by Simulations Publications, Inc. (SPI).
It was published by Kokusai-Tsushin Co. (国際通信社).
PublishedIn: It was published in the November–December 2008 issue of the Japanese magazine Command (#83).
It was published in the October 2022 edition of Command.
UsedFor: It is used for introducing newcomers to wargaming.
UsedIn: It is used in the Blue & Gray Folio Series.
WrittenBy: It was written by Tom Walczyk.
